---
description: Detect documentation drift when code changes are pushed to develop
on:
  push:
    branches: [develop]
  workflow_dispatch:

engine: claude

permissions:
  contents: read
  issues: read

tools:
  github:
    toolsets: [repos, issues]

safe-outputs:
  create-issue:
    max: 1
    title-prefix: "[docs-drift] "
    labels: [docs, auto-docs]
    close-older-issues: true
---

# Docs Drift Detection

When code is pushed to the `develop` branch, check if the changes may have caused documentation to become outdated.

## Instructions

1. Examine the commits in this push to identify changed files.

2. **Skip the entire workflow** (call `noop`) if ALL changed files fall into these categories:
   - Dependency bumps only (`package.json` version changes, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `requirements.txt`)
   - Test-only changes (files under `**/__tests__/**`, `**/*.test.*`, `**/*.spec.*`)
   - Formatting / whitespace-only changes
   - CI config changes (`.github/workflows/**`)
   - Documentation files themselves (`docs/**`, `*.md`) — these are updates, not drift sources

3. For non-skipped changes, map each changed code file to potentially affected documentation:

   | Code Path | Affected Documentation |
   |-----------|----------------------|
   | `packages/server/**`, `apps/engine/**` | `docs/` server docs, `apps/vscode/docs/api/ROCKETRIDE_QUICKSTART.md`, `apps/vscode/docs/api/ROCKETRIDE_COMMON_MISTAKES.md`, `apps/vscode/docs/api/ROCKETRIDE_README.md` |
   | `packages/client-typescript/**` | `apps/vscode/docs/api/ROCKETRIDE_typescript_API.md`, `apps/vscode/docs/api/ROCKETRIDE_COMMON_MISTAKES.md` |
   | `packages/client-python/**` | `apps/vscode/docs/api/ROCKETRIDE_python_API.md`, `apps/vscode/docs/api/ROCKETRIDE_COMMON_MISTAKES.md` |
   | `packages/client-mcp/**` | `apps/vscode/docs/api/ROCKETRIDE_python_API.md` |
   | `packages/tika/**` | `docs/` server docs |
   | `nodes/**` | `apps/vscode/docs/api/ROCKETRIDE_PIPELINE_RULES.md`, `apps/vscode/docs/api/ROCKETRIDE_COMPONENT_REFERENCE.md` |
   | `packages/ai/**` | `apps/vscode/docs/api/ROCKETRIDE_QUICKSTART.md`, AI-related docs |
   | `apps/chat-ui/**`, `apps/dropper-ui/**`, `packages/shared-ui/**` | UI documentation in `docs/` |
   | `apps/vscode/**` | `apps/vscode/docs/api/` API docs, VS Code extension docs |

4. If there are potentially affected docs, create a single issue with:
   - Title: `[docs-drift] Documentation may need updates for {brief description of changes}`
   - Body containing:

   ```
   ## Documentation Drift Detected

   Code changes in the latest push to `develop` may have caused documentation to become outdated.

   ### Changed Code
   {list of changed files grouped by module}

   ### Potentially Affected Documentation
   {list of doc files that may need updates, with brief explanation of why}

   ### Suggested Updates
   {specific suggestions for what to check or update in each doc file}

   ---
   _Automatically generated by docs-drift-detect workflow._
   ```

5. If no documentation is potentially affected after mapping (no rows matched in step 3), call `noop` and stop — do not create an issue.

6. The `close-older-issues` option is enabled, so previous unresolved `[docs-drift]` issues created by this workflow will be automatically closed when a new one is created. This prevents accumulation.

## Important Rules

- Create at most 1 issue per push event.
- Be specific about which documentation files are affected and why.
- Do not flag changes that are clearly internal implementation details with no public API or behavior impact.
- Focus on user-facing API changes, behavior changes, new features, and removed functionality.
